children.text: in the case of residential rental property (as defined in section 167(j)(2)(B)) other than that covered by clauses (i) and (ii), 100 percent minus 1 percentage point for each full month the property was held after the date the property was held 100 full months;

children.label: iv
children.text: in the case of section 1250 property with respect to which a depreciation deduction for rehabilitation expenditures was allowed under section 167(k), 100 percent minus 1 percentage point for each full month in excess of 100 full months after the date on which such property was placed in service; and

children.label: v
children.text: in the case of all other section 1250 property, 100 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: that portion of the additional depreciation attributable to periods before , in respect of the property, orJanuary 1, 1970

children.label: ii
children.text: the excess of the amount determined under paragraph (1)(A)(ii) over the sum of the amounts determined under paragraphs (1)(A)(i) and (2)(A)(i),

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: B Applicable percentage
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “applicable percentage” means 100 percent minus 1 percentage point for each full month the property was held after the date on which the property was held for 20 full months.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: 4 Special rule
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, any reference to section 167(k) or 167(j)(2)(B) shall be treated as a reference to such section as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 5 Cross reference
children.text: For reduction in the case of corporations on capital gain treatment under this section, see section 291(a)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “additional depreciation” means, in the case of any property, the depreciation adjustments in respect of such property; except that, in the case of property held more than one year, it means such adjustments only to the extent that they exceed the amount of the depreciation adjustments which would have resulted if such adjustments had been determined for each taxable year under the straight line method of adjustment.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “renewal period” means any period for which the lease may be renewed, extended, or continued pursuant to an option exercisable by the lessee, but

children.label: B
children.text: the inclusion of renewal periods shall not extend the period taken into account by more than ⅔ of the period on the basis of which the depreciation adjustments were allowed.

children.heading: Depreciation adjustments
children.label: 3 Depreciation adjustments
children.text: The term “depreciation adjustments” means, in respect of any property, all adjustments attributable to periods after , reflected in the adjusted basis of such property on account of deductions (whether in respect of the same or other property) allowed or allowable to the taxpayer or to any other person for exhaustion, wear and tear, obsolescence, or amortization (other than amortization under section 168 (as in effect before its repeal by the Tax Reform Act of 1976), 169, 185 (as in effect before its repeal by the Tax Reform Act of 1986), 188 (as in effect before its repeal by the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990), 190, or 193). For purposes of the preceding sentence, if the taxpayer can establish by adequate records or other sufficient evidence that the amount allowed as a deduction for any period was less than the amount allowable, the amount taken into account for such period shall be the amount allowed.December 31, 1963

children.heading: Additional depreciation attributable to rehabilitation expenditures
children.label: 4 Additional depreciation attributable to rehabilitation expenditures
children.text: The term “additional depreciation” also means, in the case of section 1250 property with respect to which a depreciation or amortization deduction for rehabilitation expenditures was allowed under section 167(k) (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990) or 191 (as in effect before its repeal by the Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981), the depreciation or amortization adjustments allowed under such section to the extent attributable to such property, except that, in the case of such property held for more than one year after the rehabilitation expenditures so allowed were incurred, it means such adjustments only to the extent that they exceed the amount of the depreciation adjustments which would have resulted if such adjustments had been determined under the straight line method of adjustment without regard to the useful life permitted under section 167(k) (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Revenue Reconciliation Act of 1990) or 191 (as in effect before its repeal by the Economic Recovery Tax Act of 1981).

children.heading: Method of computing straight line adjustments
children.label: 5 Method of computing straight line adjustments
A
children.text: in the case of property to which section 168 applies, by determining the adjustments which would have resulted for such year if the taxpayer had elected the straight line method for such year using the recovery period applicable to such property, and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case any property to which section 168 does not apply, if a useful life (or salvage value) was used in determining the amount allowable as a deduction for any taxable year, by using such life (or value).

children.heading: Section 1250 property
children.label: c Section 1250 property
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “section 1250 property” means any real property (other than section 1245 property, as defined in section 1245(a)(3)) which is or has been property of a character subject to the allowance for depreciation provided in section 167.

children.heading: Gifts
children.label: 1 Gifts
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to a disposition by gift.

children.heading: Transfers at death
children.label: 2 Transfers at death
children.text: Except as provided in section 691 (relating to income in respect of a decedent), subsection (a) shall not apply to a transfer at death.

children.heading: Certain tax-free transactions
children.label: 3 Certain tax-free transactions
children.text: If the basis of property in the hands of a transferee is determined by reference to its basis in the hands of the transferor by reason of the application of section 332, 351, 361, 721, or 731, then the amount of gain taken into account by the transferor under subsection (a) shall not exceed the amount of gain recognized to the transferor on the transfer of such property (determined without regard to this section). Except as provided in paragraph (6), this paragraph shall not apply to a disposition to an organization (other than a cooperative described in section 521) which is exempt from the tax imposed by this chapter.

children.heading: Like kind exchanges; involuntary conversions, etc.
children.label: 4 Like kind exchanges; involuntary conversions, etc.
i
children.text: the amount of gain recognized on the disposition (determined without regard to this section), increased as provided in subparagraph (B), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount determined under subparagraph (C).

children.heading: Increase for certain stock
children.label: B Increase for certain stock
children.text: With respect to any transaction, the increase provided by this subparagraph is the amount equal to the fair market value of any stock purchased in a corporation which (but for this paragraph) would result in nonrecognition of gain under section 1033(a)(2)(A).

children.heading: Adjustment where insufficient section 1250 property is acquired
children.label: C Adjustment where insufficient section 1250 property is acquired
i
children.text: the amount of gain which would (but for this paragraph) be taken into account under subsection (a), over

children.label: ii
children.text: the fair market value (or cost in the case of a transaction described in section 1033(a)(2)) of the section 1250 property acquired in the transaction.

children.label: i
children.text: first solely to section 1250 properties and to the amount of gain not taken into account under subsection (a) by reason of this paragraph, and

children.label: ii
children.text: then to all purchased properties to which such sentence applies and to the remaining gain not recognized on the transaction as if the cost of the section 1250 properties were the basis of such properties computed under clause (i).

children.heading: Additional depreciation with respect to property disposed of
children.label: E Additional depreciation with respect to property disposed of
children.text: In the case of any transaction described in section 1031 or 1033, the additional depreciation in respect of the section 1250 property acquired which is attributable to the section 1250 property disposed of shall be an amount equal to the amount of the gain which was not taken into account under subsection (a) by reason of the application of this paragraph.

children.heading: Property distributed by a partnership to a partner
children.label: 5 Property distributed by a partnership to a partner

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this section, the basis of section 1250 property distributed by a partnership to a partner shall be deemed to be determined by reference to the adjusted basis of such property to the partnership.

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of the gain to which subsection (a) would have applied if such property had been sold by the partnership immediately before the distribution at its fair market value at such time and the applicable percentage for the property had been 100 percent, reduced by

children.label: ii
children.text: if section 751(b) applied to any part of such gain, the amount of such gain to which section 751(b) would have applied if the applicable percentage for the property had been 100 percent.

children.heading: Transfers to tax-exempt organization where property will be used in unrelated business
children.label: 6 Transfers to tax-exempt organization where property will be used in unrelated business

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The second sentence of paragraph (3) shall not apply to a disposition of section 1250 property to an organization described in section 511(a)(2) or 511(b)(2) if, immediately after such disposition, such organization uses such property in an unrelated trade or business (as defined in section 513).

children.heading: Later change in use
children.label: B Later change in use
children.text: If any property with respect to the disposition of which gain is not recognized by reason of subparagraph (A) ceases to be used in an unrelated trade or business of the organization acquiring such property, such organization shall be treated for purposes of this section as having disposed of such property on the date of such cessation.

children.heading: Foreclosure dispositions
children.label: 7 Foreclosure dispositions
children.text: If any section 1250 property is disposed of by the taxpayer pursuant to a bid for such property at foreclosure or by operation of an agreement or of process of law after there was a default on indebtedness which such property secured, the applicable percentage referred to in paragraph (1)(B), (2)(B), or (3)(B) of subsection (a), as the case may be, shall be determined as if the taxpayer ceased to hold such property on the date of the beginning of the proceedings pursuant to which the disposition occurred, or, in the event there are no proceedings, such percentage shall be determined as if the taxpayer ceased to hold such property on the date, determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, on which such operation of an agreement or process of law, pursuant to which the disposition occurred, began.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of property acquired by the taxpayer, on the day after the date of acquisition, or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of property constructed, reconstructed, or erected by the taxpayer, on the first day of the month during which the property is placed in service.

children.heading: Property with transferred basis
children.label: 2 Property with transferred basis
children.text: If the basis of property acquired in a transaction described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3) of subsection (d) is determined by reference to its basis in the hands of the transferor, then the holding period of the property in the hands of the transferee shall include the holding period of the property in the hands of the transferor.

children.heading: Special rules for property which is substantially improved
children.label: f Special rules for property which is substantially improved

children.heading: Amount treated as ordinary income
children.label: 1 Amount treated as ordinary income
children.text: If, in the case of a disposition of section 1250 property, the property is treated as consisting of more than one element by reason of paragraph (3), then the amount taken into account under subsection (a) in respect of such section 1250 property as ordinary income shall be the sum of the amounts determined under paragraph (2).

children.heading: Ordinary income attributable to an element
children.label: 2 Ordinary income attributable to an element
A
children.text: the amount which bears the same ratio to the lower of the amounts specified in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (a)(1)(A), in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (a)(2)(A), or in clause (i) or (ii) of subsection (a)(3)(A), as the case may be, for the section 1250 property as the additional depreciation for such element attributable to such period bears to the sum of the additional depreciation for all elements attributable to such period, by

children.label: B
children.text: the applicable percentage for such element for such period.

children.heading: Property consisting of more than one element
children.label: 3 Property consisting of more than one element
A
children.text: each separate improvement,

children.label: B
children.text: if, before completion of section 1250 property, units thereof (as distinguished from improvements) were placed in service, each such unit of section 1250 property, and

children.label: C
children.text: the remaining property which is not taken into account under subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.label: i
children.text: 25 percent of the adjusted basis of the property,

children.label: ii
children.text: 10 percent of the adjusted basis of the property, determined without regard to the adjustments provided in paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 1016(a), or

children.label: ii
children.text: one percent of the adjusted basis referred to in subparagraph (A)(ii), determined, however, as of the beginning of such taxable year.

children.heading: Improvement
children.label: C Improvement
children.text: The term “improvement” means, in the case of any section 1250 property, any addition to capital account for such property after the initial acquisition or after completion of the property.

children.heading: Adjustments to basis
children.label: g Adjustments to basis
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe such regulations as he may deem necessary to provide for adjustments to the basis of property to reflect gain recognized under subsection (a).

children.heading: Application of section
children.label: h Application of section
children.text: This section shall apply notwithstanding any other provision of this subtitle.
